pattern: Serialize every folder subtree into a canonical signature, then mark for deletion any signature that describes two or more non-empty subtrees.
hints: Build the folder tree from the paths, then think about a string that uniquely encodes the shape of each subtree.
Two folders are identical when their children, recursively, produce the same canonical serialization, so compute a signature bottom-up.

Count how many times each non-empty subtree signature appears; mark every folder whose signature occurs more than once, then output the paths of all surviving (unmarked, and not under a marked ancestor) folders.

edge_inputs.description: two folders each holding an empty 'b' are duplicates
deletion is one-shot; later-identical folders survive
same leaf name in different parents is not a duplicate subtree
duplicate empty leaves at different depths get marked
deeper identical subtrees, parents differ only above

stress.description: random forest of short folder names with frequent shared subtree shapes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    names = [chr(c) for c in range(97, 107)]
    paths = set()
    roots = max(1, size // 30)
    for _ in range(roots):
        r = rng.choice(names)
        paths.add((r,))
        depth = rng.randrange(0, 4)
        cur = [r]
        for _ in range(depth):
            cur = cur + [rng.choice(names)]
            paths.add(tuple(cur))
    out = [list(p) for p in paths]
    return [out]
stress.note: constraint says parent of any non-root folder is present; building incrementally guarantees it
